creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Klaus Küchemann from comment #38)

There is another test: eliminate FreeBSD from a
copy of the media, including not having a
copy of FreeBSD's loader.efi.

U-boot should still start and if this activity
is before loading FreeBSD's loader.efi copy
(bootaa64.efi) then likely still reports the
same error for the failed drive configuration.

Of course, this media would not complete a boot,
but we would only be checking if:

ERROR: failure to add disk device usb_mass_storage.lun0, r = 20
Eeror: Cannot initialize UEFI sub-system, r = 20
1258028 bytes read in 72 ms (16.7 MiB/s)
libfdt fdt_check_header(): FDT_ERR_BADMAGIC
Error: Cannot initialize UEFI sub-system, r = 20
EFI LOAD FAILED: continuing...

happens or not or if it does not even get that
far in the message sequence.

If the messages still occur, FreeBSD would then
have been confirmed as not involved.
